# 03 - Backend Architecture

## Stack

- Flask
- Flask-CORS
- pandas
- mlxtend
- scikit-learn
- joblib

---

## Backend Structure

```
backend/
  app.py
  mining_live.py
  recommender.py
  train.py
  requirements.txt
  data/
    retail.csv
  models/
    *.pkl (generated by training)
```

---

## Module Responsibilities

### `app.py`

- Creates Flask app and enables CORS
- Registers API endpoints
- Delegates mining and model logic to service modules

### `mining_live.py`

- Parses raw CSV text
- Detects columns using flexible header matching
- Builds synthetic transaction IDs when invoice IDs are missing
- Cleans data and validates suitability
- Runs Apriori or FP-Growth (current analyze endpoint uses FP-Growth)
- Returns normalized analytics payload for frontend

### `recommender.py`

- Loads trained models and retail dataset
- Exposes model-backed methods for recommendations, rules, segments, predictions, analytics

### `train.py`

- Offline training script
- Trains Apriori, FP-Growth, KMeans, and Decision Tree artifacts
- Saves artifacts to `backend/models/`

---

## Runtime Modes

### Mode A: Live CSV Analysis (used by UI)

- No pre-trained model required
- Works directly from uploaded CSV via `/api/analyze`

### Mode B: Model-backed Endpoints

- Requires `.pkl` files in `backend/models/`
- Used by recommendation/segment/predict style endpoints

---

## Current Operational Notes

- Backend is runnable now
- Analyze flow is production-relevant for current UI
- Model endpoints are available but depend on training artifacts
- Error handling exists, but no centralized schema standard yet
